title: Disqualification: issuer/promoters/directors/sellers debarred
text: An issuer shall not be eligible to make an initial public offer if the issuer, any of its promoters, promoter group or directors or selling shareholders are debarred from accessing the capital market by the Board.

source.span_hint: debarred from accessing the capital market

title: Disqualification: promoter/director of another debarred company
text: An issuer shall not be eligible to make an initial public offer if any of the promoters or directors of the issuer is a promoter or director of any other company which is debarred from accessing the capital market by the Board.

source.span_hint: promoter or director of any other company

title: Disqualification: wilful defaulter or fraudulent borrower
text: An issuer shall not be eligible to make an initial public offer if the issuer or any of its promoters or directors is a wilful defaulter or a fraudulent borrower.

source.span_hint: wilful defaulter or a fraudulent borrower

title: Disqualification: fugitive economic offender
text: An issuer shall not be eligible to make an initial public offer if any of its promoters or directors is a fugitive economic offender.

source.span_hint: fugitive economic offender

title: Net tangible assets ≥ ₹3 crore in each of last 3 years (≤50% monetary)
text: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer only if it has net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, in each of the preceding three full years, of which not more than fifty per cent are held in monetary assets.
notes: Map to net_tangible_assets ≥ 3cr (restated, consolidated) ≤50% monetary assets.

source.span_hint: net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees

title: Operating profit ≥ ₹15 cr in each of last 3 years
text: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer only if it has an average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, during the preceding three years, with operating profit in each of these preceding three years.
notes: Map to operating_profits length=3 and each ≥ 15cr (restated, consolidated).

source.span_hint: average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees

title: Net worth ≥ ₹1 cr in each of last 3 years
text: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer only if it has a net worth of at least one crore rupees in each of the preceding three full years, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis.

source.span_hint: net worth of at least one crore rupees

title: Outstanding SARs fully exercised pre-filing
text: Outstanding stock appreciation rights granted to employees pursuant to a SAR scheme, which are fully exercised for equity shares prior to the filing of RHP (book-built issues) or prospectus (fixed price issues), with disclosures in draft offer document and offer document.

source.span_hint: outstanding stock appreciation rights

title: Fully paid-up convertible securities to be converted pre-filing
text: Fully paid-up outstanding convertible securities which are required to be converted on or before the date of filing of RHP (book-built issues) or prospectus (fixed price issues).

source.span_hint: fully paid-up outstanding convertible securities

title: Revenue ≥ 50% from new activity if name changed in last year
text: If issuer has changed its name within the last one year, at least fifty per cent. of the revenue, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, for the preceding one full year must have been earned by it from the activity indicated by its new name.
notes: Map to revenue ≥ 50% from new activity if name changed in last year.

source.span_hint: revenue, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis

title: Revenue requirement for name change within a year
text: If the issuer has changed its name within the last one year, at least fifty per cent. of the revenue, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, for the preceding one full year must have been earned by it from the activity indicated by its new name.
notes: Map to revenue_from_new_activity ≥ 50% in last year if name changed recently.

source.span_hint: if it has changed its name within the last one year

title: Book-building process and QIB allocation for non-compliance
text: An issuer not satisfying the condition stipulated in sub-regulation (1) shall be eligible to make an initial public offer only if the issue is made through the book-building process and the issuer undertakes to allot at least seventy five per cent. of the net offer to qualified institutional buyers and to refund the full subscription money if it fails to do so.
notes: Map to QIB_allocation ≥ 75% for non-compliance cases.

source.span_hint: an issuer not satisfying the condition stipulated in sub-regulation (1)

title: Technology-intensive use requirement for SR equity shares issuance
text: The issuer shall be intensive in the use of technology, information technology, intellectual property, data analytics, bio-technology or nano-technology to provide products, services or business platforms with substantial value addition.

source.span_hint: the issuer shall be intensive in the use of technology

title: Net worth limit for SR shareholder
text: The net worth of the SR shareholder, as determined by a Registered Valuer, shall not be more than rupees one thousand crore.

source.span_hint: the net worth of the SR shareholder

title: SR shares issued only to executive promoters/founders
text: The SR shares were issued only to the promoters/ founders who hold an executive position in the issuer company.

source.span_hint: The SR shares were issued only to the promoters

title: SR equity share issuance authorization requirements
text: The issue of SR equity shares had been authorized by a special resolution passed at a general meeting of the shareholders of the issuer, where the notice calling for such general meeting specifically provided for - (a) the size of issue of SR equity shares, (b) ratio of voting rights of SR equity shares vis-à-vis the ordinary shares.

source.span_hint: The issue of SR equity shares had been authorized

title: SR equity share issuance timeline requirement
text: The SR equity shares have been issued prior to the filing of draft red herring prospectus and held for a period of at least three months prior to the filing of the red herring prospectus.
notes: Map to sr_shares_held_for ≥ 3 months before RHP filing.

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares have been issued prior

title: Voting rights ratio for SR equity shares
text: The SR equity shares shall have voting rights in the ratio of a minimum of 2:1 upto a maximum of 10:1 compared to ordinary shares and such ratio shall be in whole numbers only.

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall have voting rights

title: Face value equivalence for SR and ordinary shares
text: The SR equity shares shall have the same face value as the ordinary shares.

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall have the same face value

title: Single class of SR equity shares requirement
text: The issuer shall only have one class of SR equity shares.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall only have one class of SR equity shares

title: SR equity shares equivalence to ordinary shares
text: The SR equity shares shall be equivalent to ordinary equity shares in all respects, except for having superior voting rights.

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall be equivalent to ordinary

title: Application for in-principle approval from stock exchanges
text: The issuer making an initial public offer shall ensure that it has made an application to one or more stock exchanges to seek an in-principle approval for listing of its specified securities on such stock exchanges and has chosen one of them as the designated stock exchange, in terms of Schedule XIX.

source.span_hint: it has made an application to one or more stock exchanges

title: Agreement with depository for dematerialisation
text: The issuer has entered into an agreement with a depository for dematerialization of the specified securities already issued and proposed to be issued.

source.span_hint: it has entered into an agreement with a depository

title: Dematerialisation of promoter-held securities
text: All its specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form prior to filing of the offer document.

source.span_hint: all its specified securities held by the promoters

title: Full payment or forfeiture of partly paid-up equity shares
text: All its existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up or have been forfeited.

source.span_hint: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares

title: Firm arrangements for project finance
text: Issuer must make firm financial arrangements towards seventy-five percent of the stated means of finance for a specific project proposed to be funded from issue proceeds, excluding the amount to be raised through the public issue or existing identifiable internal accruals.

source.span_hint: seventy five per cent. of the stated means

title: Limit on amount for general corporate purposes
text: The amount allocated for general corporate purposes shall not exceed twenty-five percent of the total amount being raised by the issuer.

source.span_hint: twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised

title: Limit on amount for general corporate purposes
text: The amount for general corporate purposes, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document shall not exceed twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer.

source.span_hint: amount for general corporate purposes

title: Definition of project
text: "project" means the object for which monies are proposed to be raised to cover the objects of the issue;

source.span_hint: project means the object

title: Track record of operating profit for partnership firms/LLPs
text: Adequate disclosures are made in the financial statements as required to be made by the issuer as per schedule III of the Companies Act, 2013;

source.span_hint: adequate disclosures are made

title: Track record of operating profit for partnership firms/LLPs - statutory auditor certification
text: The accounts and the disclosures made are in accordance with the provisions of schedule III of the Companies Act, 2013;

source.span_hint: accounts and the disclosures made

title: Track record of operating profit for partnership firms/LLPs - accounting standards compliance
text: The applicable accounting standards have been followed;

source.span_hint: applicable accounting standards

title: Track record of operating profit for partnership firms/LLPs - true and fair view
text: The financial statements present a true and fair view of the firm’s accounts;

source.span_hint: true and fair view of the firm’s accounts

title: Track record of distributable profits for spun-off divisions
text: In case of an issuer formed out of a division of an existing company, the track record of distributable profits of the division spun-off shall be considered only if the requirements regarding financial statements as provided for partnership firms or limited liability partnerships in Explanation (II) are complied with.

source.span_hint: track record of distributable profits

title: Limit on amount for general corporate purposes and unspecified objects
text: The amount for general corporate purposes, and such objects where the issuer company has not identified acquisition or investment target, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document, shall not exceed thirty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer.

source.span_hint: thirty five per cent

title: Limit on amount for unspecified objects
text: The amount raised for such objects where the issuer company has not identified acquisition or investment target, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document, shall not exceed twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer.

source.span_hint: twenty five per cent

title: Limit on general corporate purposes and unspecified acquisitions/investments
text: (i) General corporate purposes, and (ii) such objects where the issuer company has not identified acquisition or investment target shall not exceed thirty five per cent of the amount being raised by the issuer. Provided that the amount raised for such objects without identified targets shall not exceed twenty five per cent of the amount being raised.

source.span_hint: thirty five per cent of the amount being raised

title: Holding period for equity shares offered for sale
text: Only fully paid-up equity shares held by sellers for at least one year prior to filing of the draft offer document may be offered for sale.

source.span_hint: held by the sellers for a period of at least one year

title: Calculation of holding period for convertible securities
text: The holding period of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities and resultant equity shares together shall be considered for the purpose of calculation of one year period.

source.span_hint: holding period of such convertible securities

title: Exceptions to holding period requirement for offer for sale
text: (a) Offer for sale of equity shares by government companies or statutory authorities engaged in the infrastructure sector. (b) Shares acquired pursuant to a scheme approved by High Court, tribunal, or Central Government under sections 230 to 234 of Companies Act, 2013.

source.span_hint: in case of an offer for sale

title: Bonus issue shares offered for sale
text: Shares issued under a bonus issue on securities held for at least one year prior to filing of the draft offer document may be offered for sale, provided they are issued out of free reserves and share premium existing in books of account as at the end of the financial year preceding the filing.

source.span_hint: bonus issue on securities

title: Additional conditions for offer for sale under regulation 6(2)
text: (a) Shares offered by shareholders holding more than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding shall not exceed fifty per cent of their pre-issue shareholding. (b) Shares offered by shareholders holding less than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding shall not exceed ten per cent of pre-issue shareholding.

source.span_hint: shares offered for sale

title: Bonus issue from free reserves and share premium
text: Such specified securities being issued out of free reserves and share premium existing in the books of account as at the end of the financial year preceding the filing of draft offer document.

source.span_hint: free reserves and share premium

title: Bonus issue not from revaluation reserves or unrealized profits
text: Such equity shares not being issued by utilisation of revaluation reserves or unrealized profits.

source.span_hint: revaluation reserves or unrealized profits

title: Offer for sale by shareholders with more than 20% shareholding
text: Shares offered for sale to the public by shareholder(s) holding, individually or with persons acting in concert, more than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding of the issuer based on fully diluted basis shall not exceed fifty per cent of their pre-issue shareholding.

source.span_hint: more than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding

title: Eligibility for IPO without prior equity listing
text: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments even without making a prior public issue of its equity shares and listing thereof, provided it is not in default of payment of interest or repayment of principal amount in respect of debt instruments issued by it to the public for more than six months.
notes: Eligibility check for convertible debt IPO without prior equity listing with no defaults over 6 months.

source.span_hint: without making a prior public issue of its equity shares

title: Credit rating requirement for convertible debt IPO
text: An issuer making an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments shall obtain credit rating from at least one credit rating agency.
notes: Issuer must have a valid credit rating before issuing convertible debt instruments.

source.span_hint: credit rating agency

title: Debenture trustee requirement for convertible debt IPO
text: An issuer making an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments shall appoint at least one debenture trustee in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993.
notes: Issuer must appoint a debenture trustee as per legal requirements.

source.span_hint: debenture trustee

title: Debenture redemption reserve requirement for convertible debt IPO
text: An issuer making an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments shall create a debenture redemption reserve in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder.
notes: Issuer must establish a debenture redemption reserve as per legal requirements.

source.span_hint: debenture redemption reserve

title: Security requirements for secured convertible debt instruments
text: If the issuer proposes to create a charge or security on its assets in respect of secured convertible debt instruments, it shall ensure that: (i) such assets are sufficient to discharge the principal amount at all times; (ii) such assets are free from any encumbrance; (iii) where security is already created on such assets in favour of any existing lender or security trustee or lessor for a second or pari passu charge has been obtained and submitted to the debenture trustee before the opening of the issue; (iv) the security or asset cover shall be arrived at after reduction of the liabilities having a first or prior charge, in case the convertible debt instruments are secured by a second or subsequent charge.
notes: Issuer must ensure sufficient and unencumbered assets for security if issuing secured convertible debt instruments.

source.span_hint: security on its assets

title: Redemption of convertible debt instruments
text: The issuer shall redeem the convertible debt instruments in terms of the offer document.
notes: Issuer must redeem convertible debt instruments as per offer document.

source.span_hint: redeem the convertible

title: Positive consent required for conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments
text: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares unless the holders of such convertible debt instruments have sent their positive consent to the issuer and non-receipt of reply to any notice sent by the issuer for this purpose shall not be construed as consent for conversion.
notes: Positive consent from holders required for conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments.

source.span_hint: positive consent

title: Option to not convert convertible debt instruments above ₹10 crore
text: Where the value of the convertible portion of any listed convertible debt instruments issued by an issuer exceeds ten crore rupees and the issuer has not determined the conversion price of such convertible debt instruments at the time of making the issue, the holders of such convertible debt instruments shall be given the option of not converting the convertible portion into equity shares.
notes: Holders have an option to not convert if value exceeds ₹10 crore and conversion price is undetermined at issuance.

source.span_hint: ten crore rupees

title: Redemption of unconverted convertible debt instruments within one month
text: Where an option is to be given to the holders of the convertible debt instruments in terms of sub-regulation (2) and if one or more of such holders do not exercise the option to convert the instruments into equity share capital at a price determined in the general meeting of the shareholders, the issuer shall redeem that part of the instruments within one month from the last date by which option is to be exercised, at a price which shall not be less than its face value.
notes: Issuer must redeem unconverted convertible debt instruments within one month if holders do not exercise conversion option.

source.span_hint: one month from the last

title: Disclosure-based exemption for redemption of convertible debt instruments
text: The provision of sub-regulation (2) shall not apply if such redemption is as per the disclosures made in the offer document.
notes: Redemption does not require option if disclosed in offer document.

source.span_hint: disclosures made

title: Restrictions on issuing convertible debt instruments for financing promoter group or affiliates
text: An issuer shall not issue convertible debt instruments for financing or providing loans to, or acquiring shares of any person who is part of the promoter group or group companies. Provided that an issuer shall be eligible to issue fully convertible debt instruments for these purposes if the period of conversion of such debt instruments is less than eighteen months from the date of issue of such debt instruments.
notes: Restrictions on issuing convertible debt instruments for financing/promoter group affiliates, with an exception for fully convertible debt within 18 months.

source.span_hint: promoter group or group companies

title: Conversion of Optionally Convertible Debt Instruments
text: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares unless the holders have sent positive consent. Non-receipt of reply to any notice sent by the issuer for this purpose shall not be construed as consent.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares (1)
source.span_hint: positive consent

title: Issue of Warrants in IPO
text: An issuer shall be eligible to issue warrants in an initial public offer subject to specified conditions.

source.span_hint: eighteen months from the date

title: Warrant Tenure Limit
text: The tenure of such warrants shall not exceed eighteen months from the date of their allotment in the initial public offer.

source.span_hint: eighteen months from the date

title: Multiple Warrants per Security
text: A specified security may have one or more warrants attached to it.

source.span_hint: one or more warrants

title: Exercise Price and Upfront Payment
text: The price or formula for determination of exercise price shall be determined upfront, disclosed in the offer document, and at least 25% consideration amount based on the exercise price shall also be received upfront.

source.span_hint: twenty-five per cent. of the

title: Forfeiture for Non-Exercise
text: If warrant holder does not exercise option within three months, consideration made in respect of such warrants shall be forfeited by the issuer.

source.span_hint: three months from the date

title: Minimum Promoters’ Contribution
text: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty per cent. of the post-issue capital.

source.span_hint: twenty per cent. of the

title: Minimum Promoters’ Contribution Requirement
text: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty percent of the post-issue capital.

source.span_hint: promoters of the issuer

title: Alternative Investment for Promoter Shortfall
text: In case the post-issue shareholding of the promoters is less than twenty percent, alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India may contribute to meet the shortfall in minimum contribution as specified for the promoters, subject to a maximum of ten percent of the post-issue capital without being identified as promoter(s).

source.span_hint: alternative investment funds

title: No Promoter Requirement if No Identifiable Promoter
text: The requirement of minimum promoters’ contribution shall not apply in case an issuer does not have any identifiable promoter.

source.span_hint: no identifiable promoter

title: Promoters Contribution by Equity or Convertible Securities
text: The promoters shall contribute twenty percent as stipulated in sub-regulation (1), either by way of equity shares or by way of subscription to convertible securities.

source.span_hint: promoters shall contribute

title: Promoters Contribution at Weighted Average Price
text: In case of any issue of convertible securities which are convertible or exchangeable on different dates and if the promoters’ contribution is by way of equity shares (conversion price being pre-determined), such contribution shall not be at a price lower than the weighted average price of the equity share capital arising out of conversion of such securities.

source.span_hint: weighted average price

title: Promoters Contribution in Convertible Debt Instruments
text: In case of an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments without a prior public issue of equity shares, the promoters shall bring in a contribution of at least twenty percent of the project cost in the form of equity shares, subject to contributing at least twenty percent of the issue size from their own funds in the form of equity shares.

source.span_hint: convertible debt instruments

title: Satisfaction of Promoters Contribution Requirement
text: The promoters shall satisfy the requirements of this regulation at least one day prior to the date of opening of the issue.

source.span_hint: satisfy the requirements

title: Escrow Account for Promoters Contribution
text: Where the promoters have to subscribe to equity shares or convertible securities towards minimum promoters’ contribution, the amount of promoters’ contribution shall be kept in an escrow account with a scheduled commercial bank, which shall be released to the issuer along with the release of the issue proceeds.

source.span_hint: escrow account

title: Promoters' Contribution: Equity or Convertible Securities
text: The promoters shall contribute twenty per cent as stipulated in sub-regulation (1), either by way of equity shares or subscription to convertible securities. If the price of the equity shares allotted pursuant to conversion is not pre-determined and not disclosed, the promoters shall subscribe only to convertible securities and undertake to subscribe to equity shares upon conversion.
notes: Promoters' contribution in equity or convertibles (20%).

source.span_hint: promoters shall contribute twenty per cent

title: Promoters' Contribution: Weighted Average Price
text: In case of convertible securities with different conversion dates, if the promoters’ contribution is by way of equity shares (conversion price being pre-determined), such contribution shall not be at a price lower than the weighted average price of the equity share capital arising out of conversion.
notes: Weighted average price for contributions in equity shares.

source.span_hint: different dates and if the promoters’ contribution

title: Promoters' Contribution: Initial Public Offer of Convertible Debt Instruments
text: In case of an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments without a prior public issue of equity shares, the promoters shall contribute at least twenty per cent of the project cost in the form of equity shares and at least twenty per cent of the issue size from their own funds.
notes: Initial public offer contribution for convertible debt instruments (20%).

source.span_hint: initial public offer of convertible debt

title: Promoters' Contribution: Escrow Account
text: The amount of promoters’ contribution shall be kept in an escrow account with a scheduled commercial bank, which shall be released to the issuer along with the release of issue proceeds.

source.span_hint: escrow account with a scheduled commercial bank

title: Promoters' Contribution: Pro-Rata Release for Large Contributions
text: Where the minimum promoters’ contribution is more than one hundred crore rupees and the initial public offer is for partly paid shares, the promoters shall bring in at least one hundred crore rupees before the date of opening of the issue and the remaining amount may be brought on a pro-rata basis before the calls are made to the public.
notes: Pro-rata release for large contributions (≥ 100 cr).

source.span_hint: one hundred crore rupees and the initial public offer

title: Ineligible Securities for Promoters' Contribution
text: For computation of minimum promoters’ contribution, the following specified securities shall not be eligible: (a) acquired during preceding three years if consideration other than cash is involved or resulting from a bonus issue using revaluation reserves or unrealised profits; (b) acquired by certain entities at a price lower than public offer price.
notes: Ineligible securities for promoters' contribution computation.

source.span_hint: specified securities shall not be eligible

title: Minimum Promoters' Contribution: Ineligible Securities
text: For the computation of minimum promoters’ contribution, the following specified securities shall not be eligible:

source.reg: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: minimum promoters’ contribution

title: Ineligible Securities: Acquired During Last Three Years
text: (a) specified securities acquired during the preceding three years, if these are:

source.reg: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: (a) specified securities acquired

title: Ineligible Securities: Non-Cash Consideration or Revaluation Involved
text: (i) acquired for consideration other than cash and revaluation of assets or capitalisation of intangible assets is involved in such transaction;

source.reg: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: (i) acquired for consideration

title: Ineligible Securities: Resulting from Bonus Issue with Revaluation Reserves or Unrealised Profits
text: (ii) resulting from a bonus issue by utilisation of revaluation reserves or unrealised profits of the issuer or from bonus issue against equity shares which are ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution;

source.reg: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: (ii) resulting from a bonus issue

title: Ineligible Securities: Acquired During Last Year at Lower Price
text: (b) specified securities acquired by the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s), during the preceding one year at a price lower than the price at which specified securities are being offered to the public in the initial public offer:

source.reg: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: (b) specified securities acquired

title: Ineligible Securities: Payment of Difference to Issuer
text: (i) if the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s), as applicable, pay to the issuer the difference between the price at which the specified securities are offered in the initial public offer and the price at which the specified securities had been acquired;

source.reg: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: (i) if the promoters and alternative investment funds

title: Ineligible Securities: Scheme Under Companies Act Sections
text: (ii) if such specified securities are acquired in terms of the scheme under sections 230 to 234 of the Companies Act, 2013, as approved by a High Court or a tribunal or the Central Government, as applicable, by the promoters in lieu of business and invested capital that had been in existence for a period of more than one year prior to such approval;

source.reg: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: (ii) if such specified securities are acquired

title: Ineligible Securities: Initial Public Offer by Government Company or Statutory Authority
text: (iii) to an initial public offer by a government company, statutory authority or corporation or any special purpose vehicle set up by any of them, which is engaged in the infrastructure sector;

source.reg: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: (iii) to an initial public offer

title: Ineligible Securities: Conversion of Compulsorily Convertible Securities
text: (iv) to equity shares arising from the conversion or exchange of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities, including depository receipts, that have been held by the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s), as applicable, for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document and such fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities are converted or exchanged into equity shares prior to the filing of the offer document (i.e., red herring prospectus in case of a book built issue and)

source.reg: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: (iv) to equity shares arising

title: Minimum Promoters' Contribution (ii)
text: (ii) if such specified securities are acquired in terms of the scheme under sections 230 to 234 of the Companies Act, 2013, as approved by a High Court or a tribunal or the Central Government, as applicable, by the promoters in lieu of business and invested capital that had been in existence for a period of more than one year prior to such approval;

source.span_hint: sections 230 to 234 of the Companies Act

title: Minimum Promoters' Contribution (iii)
text: (iii) to an initial public offer by a government company, statutory authority or corporation or any special purpose vehicle set up by any of them, which is engaged in the infrastructure sector;

source.span_hint: initial public offer by a government company

title: Minimum Promoters' Contribution (iv)
text: (iv) to equity shares arising from the conversion or exchange of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities, including depository receipts, that have been held by the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s), as applicable, for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document and such fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities are converted or exchanged into equity shares prior to the filing of the offer document (i.e., red herring prospectus in case of a book built issue and prospectus in case of a fixed price issue), provided that full disclosures of the terms of conversion or exchange are made in such draft offer document;

source.span_hint: fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities

title: Eligibility for Promoters' Contribution (2)
text: (2) Specified securities referred to in clauses (a) and (c) of sub-regulation (1) shall be eligible for the computation of promoters’ contribution if such securities are acquired pursuant to a scheme which has been approved by a High Court or a tribunal or the Central Government under sections 230 to 234 of the Companies Act, 2013.

source.span_hint: Specified securities referred

title: Lock-in for securities allotted to promoters and certain entities
text: Specified securities allotted to the promoters, alternative investment funds, foreign venture capital investors, scheduled commercial banks, public financial institutions, insurance companies registered with IRDAI, or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least 5% of post-issue capital or any entity forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s) during the preceding one year at a price less than the issue price against funds brought in by them shall be locked-in for eighteen months from the date of allotment.
